# Event stream publication (US2) — Requirements quality checklist

**Purpose**: Validate clarity, completeness, and consistency of **written requirements** for asynchronous publication of session detections (not runtime behavior).  
**Created**: 2026-04-20  
**Revised**: 2026-04-22  
**Feature**: `/home/hugo/network-monitoring/specs/001-session-detection/spec.md` (User Story 2, FR-013–FR-016, SC-005)

**Note**: Generated by `/speckit.checklist`. Items question the **spec/plan wording**, not whether Kafka or the probe “works”.

---

## Requirement completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are the **preconditions for publication** (enabled vs disabled, configuration surface) stated clearly enough that FR-013 can be reviewed without inferring behavior only from `plan.md` or `tasks.md`? [Completeness, Spec FR-013]
- [x] CHK002 Is the **default destination** for session events defined in relation to a **named platform standard** that is identifiable elsewhere (spec, constitution, or linked contract), or does the requirement rely on an undefined label? [Clarity, Gap, Spec FR-013]
- [x] CHK003 Are **operational expectations for topic lifecycle** (e.g. explicit provisioning vs implicit broker defaults) captured at the specification level, or acknowledged as **out of spec scope** with a single authoritative place (plan/tasks/ADR)? [Completeness, Gap — Spec FR-013 vs Plan operational standard]

## Requirement clarity

- [x] CHK004 Is **“one event per validated session detection”** under FR-013 unambiguous with respect to **duplicate suppression** (same window and identity semantics as FR-011)? [Clarity, Consistency, Spec FR-013, FR-011]
- [x] CHK005 Does FR-014 pin **correlation key derivation** to the **same elements** as duplicate suppression without leaving room for divergent interpretations across documents? [Clarity, Spec FR-014, FR-011]
- [x] CHK006 Does FR-015 state **who is bound** by “compatible evolution” (probe only vs registry consumers vs all publishers) clearly enough for Article 7 reviews? [Clarity, Spec FR-015, Constitution Art. 7]
- [x] CHK007 Does FR-016 separate **in-transit encryption**, **production-class authentication**, and **documented non-production relaxation** into reviewable criteria (not only narrative intent)? [Clarity, Spec FR-016]
- [x] CHK008 Does **SC-005** define what **“sampled”** means for the 100% contract-match claim, or is sampling methodology only implied? [Measurability, Spec SC-005]

## Consistency & scenario coverage

- [x] CHK009 Are **US2 acceptance scenarios** consistent with **US1** on invalid observations (no publish + continued processing) without contradicting FR-004 or edge-case bullets? [Consistency, Spec US2, FR-004, Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK010 When both **operator-visible output** and **stream publication** are active, are requirements explicit about **whether each validated detection must appear on both channels** or allow product-level exceptions? [Clarity, Spec US2 preamble, FR-002, FR-013]
- [x] CHK011 Are **exception flows** for publication (e.g. stream temporarily unavailable while publication is enabled) **intentionally unspecified**, and is that absence called out as a scope boundary? [Coverage, Exception, Gap]

## Non-functional & dependencies

- [x] CHK012 Does the spec’s reliance on **“organizational security policy”** in FR-016 create **unverifiable** acceptance unless paired with documented assumptions or references? [Measurability, Ambiguity, Spec FR-016]
- [x] CHK013 Are **dependencies between** FR-015 (contract) and external **ADR/contract files** documented so readers know which artifact wins on conflict? [Traceability, Spec FR-015, Plan § ADR 0006]
- [x] CHK014 Are **ordering, idempotency, or retry semantics** for the event stream **in or out of scope** for this feature slice, and is that stated explicitly for readers? [Completeness, Gap, NFR]

## Notes

- **Revisión cerrada (2026-04-20):** Se acepta el paquete actual **spec + plan + tasks + ADRs 0006–0008 + contratos** como base suficiente para implementar US2. Huecos menores (p. ej. muestreo SC-005, reintentos) se cubren en **plan/tasks/quickstart** o se dejan explícitos al implementar.
- **Entrega US2 (código y tareas, 2026-04-22):** Las fases 5–7 de **`tasks.md` (T034–T050)** están **completas** en el repositorio (stack de referencia KRaft + Registry, tópico **`sessions.detected`**, publicación **Avro**, **SC-005** según `research.md` / quickstart). Este checklist valida requisitos **por escrito**; no sustituye evidencia de runtime.
- **Siguiente tramo de producto / implementación:** indexación de sesiones con Elasticsearch + Kafka Connect según `specs/002-session-indexing/` y `docs/adr/0009-*.md`. No modificar `specs/003-device-discovery`.
